description: A series of events that are regularly repeated in the same order. 'Nuri' names the great returns—the patterns that circle back, seasons and moons and generations, endings that become beginnings.
sound_symbolism: The word turns and returns. 'Nu' begins with grounded nasal, the starting point. Then 'ri' rolls back around, returning to begin again.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo nuri' (the cycle). Takes the abstract classifier 'nophe' when counted. Central to discussions of seasons, life stages, and natural rhythms. Related to 'meluna' (season) as one cycle among many.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional life was structured by cycles—lunar, seasonal, generational. Understanding cycles meant understanding time itself.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Cycles (samsara) are central to Buddhist teaching—the wheel of becoming that turns until wisdom breaks the pattern.
